evals.name: delegate-analytics-brief
evals.prompt: Delegate the quarterly analytics brief to Omar. He needs to produce a 10-slide deck for the exec weekly on Monday. The brief should cover Q3 performance vs targets, with recommendations for Q4 budget allocation. Omar prefers async communication and dislikes last-minute requests.
evals.expected_output: A complete delegation record with specific deliverable (10-slide deck), named owner (Omar), specific deadline (before Monday exec weekly), context (Q3 performance for exec audience), handoff communication drafted in Omar's preferred async style, and follow-up plan with confirmation window and escalation triggers.
expectations: Delegation record includes all seven quality checklist items: specific deliverable, named person, specific deadline, context with purpose and audience, format specified, delegatee's preferred style, and follow-up mechanism
Handoff communication is drafted in Omar's preferred async style (Slack or email, not a meeting request) and acknowledges any time pressure with an offer to adjust scope
Follow-up plan includes a confirmation window (24 hours for same-week items), midpoint check-in, and overdue handling protocol
A delegation log entry is proposed for work.local.md with all required fields including confirmed: false and status: PENDING CONFIRMATION

evals.name: delegation-audit-open-items
evals.prompt: Show me all my open delegations. I want to see what I have delegated, to whom, what is overdue, and what needs follow-up this week.
evals.expected_output: A structured view of all open delegations from the delegation log in work.local.md, showing each delegation's status, owner, due date, and whether it is overdue or needs follow-up, with specific recommended actions for overdue items.
expectations: Output loads the delegation log from work.local.md and presents all entries where status is not COMPLETE
Overdue delegations are flagged prominently with specific follow-up recommendations based on how long they are overdue (1 day, 3 days, 1 week thresholds)
Delegations awaiting confirmation are highlighted separately from those in progress
Output includes a recommended next action for each open delegation

evals.name: negative-should-use-task-intelligence
evals.prompt: Brain dump: I need to finish the analytics brief, chase Omar on the vendor contract, prep for Monday exec weekly, and review the Nighthawk facility lease. Sort these by priority.
evals.expected_output: This query should be handled by task-intelligence, not delegation. Brain dump capture and P1/P2/P3 prioritisation is a task-intelligence function.
expectations: This query is about capturing unstructured items and prioritising them — it matches task-intelligence triggers (brain dump, prioritise)
Delegation is for creating specific delegation records with handoff communication, not for sorting a personal to-do list

evals.name: negative-should-use-task-management
evals.prompt: Add a task to my list: review the Q3 analytics report by Friday. Mark it as high priority.
evals.expected_output: This query should be handled by the official productivity plugin's task-management, not delegation. Basic task CRUD is the official plugin's responsibility.
expectations: This query is about adding a task to a personal task list — it matches official productivity plugin task-management triggers (add task)
Delegation is for delegating work to others with handoff communication and follow-up, not for personal task management
